summary: Backend testing of Security Dashboard v2: whitelist + notification toggles + Telegram Bot integration + notifications/test endpoint. Created /app/backend/tests/test_security_whitelist_and_telegram.py with 19 tests (8 pure unit tests for _ip_in_whitelist + 11 HTTP integration tests). All 19 pass reliably (verified across 3 consecutive runs). Regressions on test_diagnostics_and_security.py (20) also pass reliably. Uncovered a PRE-EXISTING xdist race between test_recaptcha_auth.py and test_login_analytics.py (flaky in ~40% of runs even WITHOUT my new file) — cause: pytest.ini uses --dist loadscope, which does NOT honor xdist_group() markers; both files racing on integration_settings.recaptcha.

minor.issue: The review spec mentions 'whitelist_ips (array or newline-string coerced)', but routes.py only iterates the value assuming it is already a list. Passing a single string like '10.0.0.0/8\n192.168.1.1' would iterate over characters. If newline-string coercion is a target UX (multi-line textarea from the UI), the coercion should be added on the backend (or the UI should split before PUT). Not a blocker — arrays work correctly.

action_items: PRE-EXISTING FLAKE (not caused by this iteration): test_recaptcha_auth.py + test_login_analytics.py race on integration_settings.recaptcha. Root cause: pytest.ini uses `--dist loadscope`, which pins by class/module but IGNORES pytest.mark.xdist_group. Both files rely on that marker to serialize but it is a no-op under loadscope. Fix options: (a) switch pytest.ini to `--dist loadgroup`, or (b) merge the two suites into one class/module so loadscope groups them, or (c) rename the marker to a `mutex`-style fixture that awaits a file lock.

OPTIONAL — backend: accept whitelist_ips as a newline-separated string in PUT /admin/security/settings (currently only accepts a list). E.g. `if isinstance(v, str): v = [x.strip() for x in v.splitlines() if x.strip()]` before the list-comprehension coercion at routes.py L4001.
critical_code_review_comments: SCHEDULING BUG (pre-existing, high-priority): /app/backend/pytest.ini declares `--dist loadscope`, but multiple test files rely on `pytestmark = pytest.mark.xdist_group('recaptcha_shared')` to serialize. loadscope does NOT honor xdist_group — only `--dist loadgroup` does. Result: test_recaptcha_auth.py and test_login_analytics.py can (and do) run on separate workers concurrently and race on integration_settings.recaptcha (~40% flakiness on repeat runs). Confirmed by 5 consecutive runs WITHOUT the new test file: 2/5 failed with the exact same symptom.

GOOD — /app/backend/portal/routes.py::_ip_in_whitelist correctly uses Python's ipaddress module for CIDR matching (both v4 and v6). Unit tests: 10.5.6.7 ∈ 10.0.0.0/8 True; 192.168.1.1 ∉ 10.0.0.0/8 False; 127.0.0.1 exact True; 2001:db8::1 ∈ 2001:db8::/32 True; hostname-exact fallback works.

GOOD — /app/backend/portal/routes.py::_is_ip_blocked short-circuits on whitelist (line 319) BEFORE the DB lookup — verified by test_06 (manual block on 127.0.0.1 still allows login while 127.0.0.1 is whitelisted).

GOOD — /admin/security/notifications/test fires both channels asynchronously and NEVER raises 5xx even when Telegram returns a 401 for a fake token (verified with bot_token='fake:key'). Response shape is stable: {email:{attempted, reason?, ok?, errors?, sent_to?}, telegram:{attempted, reason?, ok?, errors?, details?}}.
GOOD — TelegramNotifier: catches network/HTTP exceptions inside .send() and returns {ok:false, message}, so the caller can surface it as ok:false without propagating a 500.

SUGGESTION — /app/backend/portal/routes.py::security_settings_put (L3985–4003): whitelist_ips coercion assumes list input. Consider also handling a string (newline-split) to match the 'array or newline-string coerced' spec in the review request.

SUGGESTION — /app/backend/portal/integrations_v2.py::redact masks any credential key containing secret/password/key/token/api_key. bot_token gets masked correctly. chat_id is NOT masked (it does not match any of the sensitive substrings) — verified with GET /admin/integrations-v2 returning creds.chat_id='-100999888' as-is. If Telegram chat_ids should be treated as semi-sensitive, add 'chat_id' to the mask list.

success_rate.backend: 100% for the new suite (19/19 passing, stable across 3 consecutive runs). Combined with test_diagnostics_and_security.py: 39/39 stable. The wider regression (adding test_recaptcha_auth.py + test_login_analytics.py) is 78/79 to 79/79 depending on run — the pre-existing loadscope race, not this iteration's changes.

seed_data_creation: None. Uses existing seeded admin. Class teardown wipes blocked_ips, wipes 127.0.0.1 failed login_attempts, deletes telegram integration, and resets security settings (auto_block=False + whitelist_ips=[] + notify flags True) to match the invariant left by test_diagnostics_and_security.py.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 1) MY NEW SUITE (test_security_whitelist_and_telegram.py) IS STABLE — 3/3 runs green. 2) PRE-EXISTING FLAKE — test_recaptcha_auth.py and test_login_analytics.py race under `--dist loadscope` because xdist_group is a no-op with loadscope. Adding `--dist loadgroup` in pytest.ini would fix it BUT you must first verify all currently-passing suites work under loadgroup (they should, since loadgroup is stricter about grouping). 3) TELEGRAM INTEGRATION uses api.telegram.org — with fake tokens it returns {ok:false, description:'Not Found'} inside a 401/200 body (Telegram sometimes 200-with-error). Our TelegramNotifier catches that and returns ok:false, so callers never see a 500. 4) Whitelist end-of-test invariant: I leave whitelist_ips=[] and auto_block_enabled=False at teardown so subsequent files (recaptcha/login_analytics) don't get IP-blocked from residual state. If a future suite needs auto_block ON, PUT /admin/security/settings first.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bug introduced by this iteration. Documented pre-existing race in action_items and critical_code_review_comments.
